NAME

session.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. __attribute__ ((unused))
Receive NETWORK_QUALITY packet from ACDS relay. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Function Documentation

__attribute__ ((unused) )

Receive NETWORK_QUALITY packet from ACDS relay.

Parameters

Session Discovery session
timeout_ms Timeout in milliseconds

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK if received, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT if no data

Attempts to receive peer's NETWORK_QUALITY from ACDS. Stores result in session->negotiate.peer_quality

Definition at line 422 of file src/discovery/session.c.

422                                                                                                                  {
423   if (!session || !session->acds_transport) {
424     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "invalid session or ACDS transport");
425   }
426 
427   log_info("★ RECEIVE_NETWORK_QUALITY: waiting for packet from ACDS...");
428   // Receive packet
429   packet_type_t ptype;
430   void *data = NULL;
431   size_t len = 0;
432   void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
433   asciichat_error_t result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &ptype, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
434 
435   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
436     log_error("★ RECEIVE_NETWORK_QUALITY_FAILED: packet_receive_via_transport returned %d: %s", result,
437               asciichat_error_string(result));
438     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
439     return result;
440   }
441 
442   log_info("★ RECEIVE_NETWORK_QUALITY_GOT_PACKET: type=%u, len=%zu", ptype, len);
443   // Check if it's a NETWORK_QUALITY packet
444   if (ptype != PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_NETWORK_QUALITY) {
445     log_warn("★ RECEIVE_NETWORK_QUALITY_WRONG_TYPE: got type %u (expected %u)", ptype, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_NETWORK_QUALITY);
446     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
447     return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
448   }
449 
450   // Parse NETWORK_QUALITY
451   if (len < sizeof(acip_nat_quality_t)) {
452     log_error("NETWORK_QUALITY packet too small: %zu bytes", len);
453     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
454     return ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE;
455   }
456 
457   acip_nat_quality_t *wire_quality = (acip_nat_quality_t *)data;
458 
459   // Convert from wire format
460   nat_quality_from_acip(wire_quality, &session->negotiate.peer_quality);
461   session->negotiate.peer_quality_received = true;
462 
463   log_debug("Received NETWORK_QUALITY from peer (NAT tier: %d, upload: %u Kbps)",
464             nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality), session->negotiate.peer_quality.upload_kbps);
465 
466   SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
467   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
468 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Become the host (called when elected as future host).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2298 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2298                                                                               {
2299   if (!session) {
2300     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2301     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2302   }
2303 
2304   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2305            session->participant_id[1]);
2306 
2307   // Get configured port (or use default)
2308   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2309 
2310   // Mark ourselves as host
2311   session->is_host = true;
2312 
2313   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2314   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2315     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2316         .port = host_port,
2317         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2318         .max_clients = 32,
2319         .encryption_enabled = true,
2320     };
2321 
2322     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2323     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2324       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2325       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2326     }
2327 
2328     // Start listening for new participant connections
2329     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2330     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2331       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2332       return hstart;
2333     }
2334 
2335     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2336   }
2337 
2338   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2339   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2340   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2341   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2342   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2343   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2344   // Set connection type based on NAT quality (for now use DIRECT_PUBLIC for localhost dev environments)
2345   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2346 
2347   if (session->acds_transport) {
2348     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
2349                               sizeof(announcement), 0);
2350     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2351              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2352   }
2353 
2354   // Mark migration complete
2355   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2356 
2357   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2358   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2359 
2360   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2361 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2175 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2175                                                                                    {
2176   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2177     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2178     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2179   }
2180 
2181   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2182   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2183     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2184     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2185       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2186     }
2187     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2188     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2189   }
2190 
2191   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2192   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2193   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2194     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2195   }
2196 
2197   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2198   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2199     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2200   }
2201 
2202   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2203 
2204   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2205   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2206     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2207     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2208       // Ping timed out
2209       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2210       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2211       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2212                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2213 
2214       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2215       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2216         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2217         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2218       }
2219     }
2220   }
2221 
2222   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2223   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2224   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2225     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2226     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2227     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2228       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2229       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2230         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2231         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2232         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2233                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2234       } else {
2235         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2236         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2237         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2238           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2239           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2240         }
2241       }
2242     }
2243   }
2244 
2245   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2246 }

2363                                                                                          {

2366     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2367   }
2368 
2369   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,

2371

2374     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2375     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2376   }
2377 
2378   // Create new participant context for the future host
2379   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2380       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2381       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2382       .enable_audio = true,
2383       .enable_video = true,
2384       .encryption_enabled = true,
2385   };

2387   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2388   if (!session->participant_ctx) {

2390     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2391   }
2392 
2393   // Attempt connection to future host
2394   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
2395   if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2396     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2397     return pconn;
2398   }
2399 
2400   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2401 
2402   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2403   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2404   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2405   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2406 
2407   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2408            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2409 
2410   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2411   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2412 
2413   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2414   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2415 
2416   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2417 }

discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const
  discovery_config_t * config)


Create a new discovery session.


Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error


Definition at line 50 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


50                                                                                 {
51   if (!config) {
52     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
53     return NULL;
54   }
55 
56   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
57   if (!session) {
58     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
59     return NULL;
60   }
61 
62   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
63   session->acds_transport = NULL;
64   session->peer_manager = NULL;
65   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
66   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
67   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
68   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
69   session->stun_servers = NULL;
70   session->stun_count = 0;
71   session->turn_servers = NULL;
72   session->turn_count = 0;
73 
74   // Initialize host liveness detection
75   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
76   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
77   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
78   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
79   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
80   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
81   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
82 
83   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
84            session->host_ctx);
85 
86   // Load or generate identity key
87   char identity_path[256];
88   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
89   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
90     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
91     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
92       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
93       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
94       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
95       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
96         // Save for future use
97         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       }
99     }
100   }
101 
102   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
103     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
104     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
105     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
106   } else {
107     char fingerprint[65];
108     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
109     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
110   }
111 
112   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
113   const options_t *opts = options_get();
114   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
115     // Load all identity keys from options
116     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
117       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
118       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
119       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
120       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
121         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
122         char fingerprint[65];
123         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
124         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
125       } else {
126         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
127       }
128     }
129     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
130     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
131   } else {
132     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
133     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
134     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
135   }
136 
137   // ACDS connection info
138   if (config->acds_url && config->acds_url[0]) {
139     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_url, config->acds_url, sizeof(session->acds_url));
140   }
141   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
142     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
143   } else {
144     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
145   }
146   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
147 
148   // Session string (if joining)
149   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
150     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
151     session->is_initiator = false;
152   } else {
153     session->is_initiator = true;
154   }
155 
156   // Callbacks
157   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
158   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
159   session->on_error = config->on_error;
160   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
161   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
162   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
163 
164   // Initialize mutex for NAT quality protection
165   if (mutex_init(&session->nat_quality_mutex, "discovery_nat_quality") != 0) {
166     log_warn("Failed to initialize NAT quality mutex");
167   }
168 
169   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
170             session->acds_port);
171 
172   return session;
173 }

177     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");

179   }
180 
181   // Close ACDS connection
182   if (session->acds_transport) {
183     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
184     session->acds_transport = NULL;
185   }
186 
187   // Destroy host context if active
188   if (session->host_ctx) {
189     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
190     session->host_ctx = NULL;
191   }
192 
193   // Destroy participant context if active
194   if (session->participant_ctx) {
195     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
196     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
197   }
198 
199   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
200   if (session->peer_manager) {
201     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
202     session->peer_manager = NULL;
203   }
204 
205   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
206   if (session->stun_servers) {
207     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
208     session->stun_servers = NULL;
209     session->stun_count = 0;
210   }
211 
212   if (session->turn_servers) {
213     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
214     session->turn_servers = NULL;
215     session->turn_count = 0;
216   }
217 
218   // Wait for NAT detection thread to complete if still running (with 500ms timeout)
219   if (session->nat_detection_thread_started) {
220     log_debug("Waiting for NAT detection thread to complete (500ms timeout)...");
221     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&session->nat_detection_thread, NULL, 500 * 1000 * 1000); // 500ms
222     if (join_result != 0) {
223       log_warn("NAT detection thread did not exit within 500ms, continuing cleanup anyway");
224     }
225     session->nat_detection_thread_started = false;
226   }
227 
228   // Destroy NAT quality mutex
229   mutex_destroy(&session->nat_quality_mutex);
230 
231   SAFE_FREE(session);
232 }

  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2248 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2248                                                                                                                      {
2249   if (!session) {
2250     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2251     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2252   }
2253 
2254   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2255 
2256   // Initialize migration context
2257   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2258   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2259   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2260   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2261 
2262   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2263   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2264   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2265   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2266   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2267   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2268   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2269 
2270   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2271 
2272   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2273   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2274     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2275     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2276     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2277     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2278     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2279   }
2280 
2281   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2282   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2283   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2284 
2285   // Check if I am the future host
2286   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2287     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2288     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2289     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2290   } else {
2291     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2292     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2293              session->ring.future_host_port);
2294     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2295   }
2296 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1417 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1417                                                                                               {
1418   if (!session) {
1419     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1420   }
1421 
1422   // Handle state-specific processing
1423   switch (session->state) {
1424   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1425     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1426     // Note: don't use a hard timeout here - let the outer process timeout (snapshot delay) control max wait
1427     // The discovery_run() loop will keep calling this until session becomes active or process exits
1428     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
1429     uint64_t wait_duration = now_ns - session->waiting_peer_start_ns;
1430 
1431     log_debug("★ WAITING_PEER: duration=%.3fs (no timeout, waiting for peer...)", wait_duration / 1e9);
1432 
1433     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1434       // Do not set socket timeout - let receive_packet_secure use its own timeout
1435       // Setting a short socket timeout conflicts with its hardcoded 1-second timeout
1436       packet_type_t type;
1437       void *data = NULL;
1438       size_t len = 0;
1439       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1440 
1441       // Try to receive a packet
1442       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1443           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1444       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1445         if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1446           log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1447           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1448           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1449         } else {
1450           // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1451           log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1452           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1453         }
1454       } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1455         // Timeout is normal, just return
1456       } else {
1457         log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1458       }
1459     } else {
1460       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1461       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1462     }
1463     break;
1464   }
1465 
1466   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1467     // Handle NAT negotiation
1468     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1469       // Initialize negotiation
1470       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1471       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1472 
1473       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS so peers can evaluate us
1474       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1475       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1476         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1477       }
1478 
1479       // ACDS relays NETWORK_QUALITY only to OTHER participants, not back to sender
1480       // For now in discovery mode, assume we're the only one and become host immediately
1481       // A real implementation would wait for peer to join via PARTICIPANT_JOINED, then connect directly
1482       log_info("Proceeding as host (ACDS cannot relay peer quality in solo mode)");
1483       session->negotiate.our_quality.detection_complete = true;
1484       session->negotiate.peer_quality_received = true;
1485       memset(&session->negotiate.peer_quality, 0, sizeof(session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1486       // Mark peer quality as detected but with worst NAT type so we win host election
1487       session->negotiate.peer_quality.detection_complete = true;
1488       session->negotiate.peer_quality.nat_type = ACIP_NAT_TYPE_SYMMETRIC;
1489     }
1490 
1491     // Check for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS (in case we're waiting for host discovery)
1492     if (session->acds_transport && !session->is_initiator && !session->host_address[0]) {
1493       packet_type_t type;
1494       void *data = NULL;
1495       size_t len = 0;
1496       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1497 
1498       asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1499       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT) {
1500         acip_host_announcement_t *announcement = (acip_host_announcement_t *)data;
1501         log_info("Received HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS: %s:%u", announcement->host_address, announcement->host_port);
1502         SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, announcement->host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1503         session->host_port = announcement->host_port;
1504         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1505         // Continue to host role determination below
1506       } else if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1507         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1508       }
1509     }
1510 
1511     // Determine host role (now that peer_quality is set)
1512     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1513       log_info("Determining host role based on NAT qualities...");
1514       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1515       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1516         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1517 
1518         if (session->is_host) {
1519           // We become the host
1520           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1521                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1522                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1523           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1524           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1525           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1526 
1527           // Fallback to localhost if NAT detection failed to determine address
1528           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1529             log_warn("NAT address empty, using localhost fallback");
1530             SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->host_address));
1531           }
1532 
1533           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1534         } else {
1535           // They are host (shouldn't happen in solo mode, but handle it)
1536           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1537                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1538                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1539                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1540           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1541           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1542 
1543           // If host address is empty, wait for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS instead of using fallback
1544           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1545             log_info("Waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS to discover host address...");
1546             // Stay in NEGOTIATING state to continue waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT
1547             break;
1548           }
1549 
1550           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1551         }
1552       } else {
1553         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1554         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1555       }
1556     }
1557     break;
1558   }
1559 
1560   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1561     // Start hosting
1562     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1563       // Create host context with configured port
1564       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1565 
1566       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1567           .port = host_port,
1568           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1569           .max_clients = 32,
1570           .encryption_enabled = true,
1571       };
1572 
1573       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1574       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1575         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1576         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1577         break;
1578       }
1579 
1580       // Start listening
1581       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1582       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1583         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1584         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1585         break;
1586       }
1587 
1588       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1589     }
1590 
1591     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1592     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1593       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1594     }
1595 
1596     // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so participants can discover us
1597     int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1598     acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
1599     memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1600     memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->host_id, 16);
1601     SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
1602     announcement.host_port = host_port;
1603     announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
1604 
1605     if (session->acds_transport) {
1606       packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
1607                                 sizeof(announcement), 0);
1608       log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS at %s:%u", announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
1609     }
1610 
1611     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1612     break;
1613   }
1614 
1615   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1616     // Connect to host as participant
1617 
1618     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1619     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1620       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1621       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1622         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1623         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1624         break;
1625       }
1626 
1627       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1628       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1629         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1630                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1631 
1632         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1633         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1634             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1635 
1636         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1637           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1638           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1639           break;
1640         }
1641 
1642         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1643         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1644         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = APP_CALLBACK_UINT64(platform_get_monotonic_time_us) / 1000;
1645       }
1646 
1647       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1648       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1649         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1650         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1651 
1652         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1653           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1654                     timeout_ms);
1655 
1656           // Check if we have retries remaining
1657           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1658           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1659             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1660             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1661 
1662             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1663                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1664 
1665             // Wait for backoff period
1666             APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
1667             APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, backoff_ms);
1668 
1669             // Destroy old peer manager
1670             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1671             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1672 
1673             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1674             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1675             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1676               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1677               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1678 
1679               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1680               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1681                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1682                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1683                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1684               }
1685 
1686               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1687             }
1688 
1689             // Reset connection state for retry
1690             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1691             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1692 
1693             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1694                      max_attempts + 1);
1695           } else {
1696             // No more retries - connection failed
1697             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1698 
1699             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1700             char error_msg[256];
1701             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1702                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1703             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1704 
1705             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1706             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1707               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1708               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1709               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1710             }
1711 
1712             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1713           }
1714         }
1715       }
1716 
1717       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1718       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1719       if (session->acds_transport && acip_transport_has_pending_data(session->acds_transport)) {
1720         packet_type_t type;
1721         void *data = NULL;
1722         size_t len = 0;
1723         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1724 
1725         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1726             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1727 
1728         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1729           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1730           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1731           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1732         } else {
1733           log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1734         }
1735       }
1736 
1737       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1738       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1739       break;
1740     }
1741 
1742     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1743       // Create participant context for this session
1744       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1745           .address = session->host_address,
1746           .port = session->host_port,
1747           .enable_audio = true,
1748           .enable_video = true,
1749           .encryption_enabled = true,
1750       };
1751 
1752       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1753       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1754         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1755         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1756         break;
1757       }
1758 
1759       // Attempt connection
1760       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1761       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1762         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1763         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1764         break;
1765       }
1766 
1767       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1768 
1769       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1770       // Get the socket from participant context
1771       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1772       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1773         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1774         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1775         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1776         break;
1777       }
1778 
1779       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1780       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1781       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1782       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1783       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1784       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1785       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1786       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1787       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1788 
1789       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1790       if (result != 0) {
1791         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1792         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1793         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1794         break;
1795       }
1796 
1797       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1798       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1799       packet_type_t packet_type;
1800       void *payload = NULL;
1801       size_t payload_len = 0;
1802 
1803       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1804       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1805         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1806         if (payload) {
1807           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1808         }
1809         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1810         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1811         break;
1812       }
1813 
1814       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1815         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1816         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1817         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1818         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1819         break;
1820       }
1821 
1822       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1823       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1824       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1825 
1826       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1827         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1828         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1829         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1830         break;
1831       }
1832 
1833       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1834                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1835 
1836       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1837       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1838       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1839       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1840       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1841       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1842       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1843       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1844       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1845       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1846 
1847       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1848       if (result != 0) {
1849         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1850         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1851         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1852         break;
1853       }
1854 
1855       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1856       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1857     }
1858 
1859     // Check if connection is established
1860     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1861     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1862     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1863               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1864 
1865     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1866       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1867       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1868     }
1869 
1870     if (is_connected) {
1871       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1872       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1873     } else {
1874       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1875                 "is_connected=%d)",
1876                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1877     }
1878     break;
1879   }
1880 
1881   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1882     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1883     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport &&
1884         acip_transport_has_pending_data(session->acds_transport)) {
1885       packet_type_t type;
1886       void *data = NULL;
1887       size_t len = 0;
1888       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1889 
1890       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1891           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1892 
1893       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1894         // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1895         handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1896         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1897       }
1898     }
1899 
1900     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1901     if (!session->is_host) {
1902       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1903       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1904       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1905         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1906         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
1907 
1908         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
1909         const options_t *opts = options_get();
1910         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
1911           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1912           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1913           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1914           return ERROR_NETWORK;
1915         }
1916 
1917         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
1918         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
1919         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
1920       }
1921     }
1922     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1923     break;
1924 
1925   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
1926     // Host migration in progress
1927     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
1928     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
1929       // Migration completed successfully
1930       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1931       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
1932     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
1933       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
1934       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
1935       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1936       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1937     }
1938     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1939     break;
1940 
1941   case DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED:
1942     // Session has failed - return error on further processing
1943     return session->error != ASCIICHAT_OK ? session->error : ERROR_NETWORK;
1944 
1945   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED:
1946     // Session has ended gracefully - return OK
1947     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1948 
1949   case DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT:
1950   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS:
1951   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION:
1952   case DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION:
1953     // These states are handled during discovery_session_start(), not in the process loop
1954     // If we reach here, something went wrong - sleep and wait
1955     log_debug("discovery_session_process: State %d should be handled by discovery_session_start", session->state);
1956     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1957     break;
1958 
1959   default:
1960     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state: %d", session->state);
1961   }
1962 
1963   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1964 }






References discovery_session_t::acds_transport,
    ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC, acip_host_announcement_t,
    ACIP_NAT_TYPE_SYMMETRIC, APP_CALLBACK_UINT64,

    nat_quality_t::nat_type, discovery_session_t::negotiate,

    NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING, NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT,
    NET_TO_HOST_U16, discovery_session_t::on_session_ready,

    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT,
    PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED,
    PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
    discovery_session_t::participant_ctx,
    discovery_session_t::participant_id,
    discovery_session_t::peer_manager,
    negotiate_ctx_t::peer_quality,
    negotiate_ctx_t::peer_quality_received,
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    platform_sleep_us(), options_state::prefer_webrtc,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_auth,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_cipher,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_kex,

    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_version, receive_packet(),

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1228 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1228                                                                         {
1229   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1230 
1231   if (!session) {
1232     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1233   }
1234 
1235   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1236            session->session_string);
1237 
1238   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1239   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1240     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1241     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1242   }
1243 
1244   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1245   // Connect to ACDS
1246   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1247   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1248     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1249     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1250     return result;
1251   }
1252   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1253 
1254   // Check again after connection
1255   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1256     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1257     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1258   }
1259 
1260   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1261   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1262   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1263   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1264 
1265   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1266   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1267     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1268     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1269     return result;
1270   }
1271 
1272   // Store identity key in handshake context
1273   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1274   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1275   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1276   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1277 
1278   // Step 0: Send protocol version to ACDS
1279   log_debug("Sending protocol version to ACDS...");
1280   protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1281   client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);
1282   client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0);
1283   client_version.supports_encryption = 1;
1284   client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1285   client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1286   client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1287 
1288   result = packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, &client_version,
1289                                      sizeof(client_version), 0);
1290   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1291     log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to send protocol version to ACDS");
1292     set_error(session, result, "Failed to send protocol version to ACDS");
1293     return result;
1294   }
1295 
1296   // Step 0.5: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1297   {
1298     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1299     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1300     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1301     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1302 
1303     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1304     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1305       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1306       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1307       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1308                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1309       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1310     }
1311 
1312     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1313       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1314                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1315       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1316       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1317       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1318     }
1319 
1320     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1321     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1322     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1323 
1324     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1325     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1326     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1327     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1328       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1329       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1330       return result;
1331     }
1332     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1333   }
1334 
1335   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1336   {
1337     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1338     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1339     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1340     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1341 
1342     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1343     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1344       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1345       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1346       return result;
1347     }
1348 
1349     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1350     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1351 
1352     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1353       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1354       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1355       return result;
1356     }
1357   }
1358 
1359   // Step 2: Receive CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE from server to finalize handshake
1360   {
1361     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1362     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1363     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1364     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1365 
1366     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1367     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1368       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1369       set_error(session, result, "Failed to receive handshake complete");
1370       return result;
1371     }
1372 
1373     if (pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE) {
1374       log_error("discovery_session_start: Expected CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, got packet type %d", pkt_type);
1375       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1376       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid crypto handshake completion packet");
1377       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1378     }
1379 
1380     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1381   }
1382 
1383   // For ACDS, handshake completes after key exchange (no auth required)
1384   log_info("discovery_session_start: ACDS handshake complete");
1385   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1386 
1387   // Check again after handshake
1388   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1389     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1390     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1391   }
1392 
1393   // Create or join session
1394   if (session->is_initiator) {
1395     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1396     result = create_session(session);
1397     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1398              session->participant_ctx, result);
1399 
1400     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1401       // Initiator also needs to register itself as a participant so it can receive PARTICIPANT_JOINED notifications
1402       // when other peers join. This makes the initiator discoverable to other participants.
1403       log_info("Initiator: now registering self as participant in created session");
1404       result = join_session(session);
1405       log_info("Initiator: join_session result=%d", result);
1406     }
1407     return result;
1408   } else {
1409     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1410     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1411     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1412              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1413     return result;
1414   }
1415 }

void
  discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t * session)


Stop the discovery session.


Parameters


Session Session context

1966                                                           {
1967   if (!session) {
1968     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1969     return;
1970   }
1971 
1972   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
1973 
1974   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
1975   if (session->acds_transport && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
1976     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
1977     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));
1978     memcpy(leave_msg.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1979     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1980 
1981     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg),
1982                               0);
1983   }
1984 
1985   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED);
1986 }
